title: Saudi-Arabian Tagine with Colby Jack Cheese
description: A flavorful and exotic Middle Eastern-inspired dish featuring tender chicken and melted colby jack cheese, served in a copper tagine pot. This flexitarian-friendly recipe is perfect for a romantic date night at home.
instructions: Heat olive oil in the copper tagine over medium-high heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the boneless chicken thighs and cook until browned, about 5-6 minutes.
Add the cumin, coriander, and tahini, and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer the mixture, covered, for 15-20 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
Stir in the chopped colby jack cheese until melted and well combined.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Garnish with chopped parsley and serve hot.
Serve the Saudi-Arabian tagine over couscous or rice, if desired.
Refrigerate leftovers for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
Thaw frozen tagine overnight in the refrigerator before reheating.
